# Extractor Receipt

## Summary

This source contains firsthand comparative benchmark evidence about four AI coding agents, including setup details, task design, prompt counts, qualitative observations from specific coding tasks, and a final scored benchmark table.

## Evidence Files

### file: evidence-01.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-21-014
evidence_id: evidence-01
kind: method
citation: Render Blog, Aug. 12, 2025
source_url: https://render.com/blog/ai-coding-agents-benchmark
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
The comparison tested four tools: Claude Code, Cursor using Claude Sonnet 4, Gemini CLI, and OpenAI Codex.

## Source Excerpt

"As of mid-2025, there are more code agent AI tools than I can count; however, I selected the following four." The source then lists "Claude Code," "Cursor (using Claude Sonnet 4 for all tests)," "Gemini CLI," and "OpenAI Codex."

## Note

The source frames these four as a selected sample rather than an exhaustive market survey.
```

### file: evidence-02.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-21-014
evidence_id: evidence-02
kind: claim-support
citation: Render Blog, Aug. 12, 2025
source_url: https://render.com/blog/ai-coding-agents-benchmark
confidence: medium
mechanism_relevance: direct
contradiction_relevance: none
---
The source says Cursor uses a local-filesystem RAG-like system to gather codebase context, which could give it an advantage.

## Source Excerpt

"A point of interest: Cursor utilizes a RAG-like(Retrieval-Augmented Generation) system on the local filesystem to gather more context on the codebase, potentially giving it an advantage."

## Note

This is a source-stated product characteristic and possible advantage, not a separately verified measurement in the article.
```

### file: evidence-03.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-21-014
evidence_id: evidence-03
kind: metric
citation: Render Blog, Aug. 12, 2025
source_url: https://render.com/blog/ai-coding-agents-benchmark
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
The source states Gemini CLI used the Pro 2.5 model and had a 1M-token context window, the largest among the tested models.

## Source Excerpt

"Gemini CLI : Google’s offering in the code agent space offers the solid Pro 2.5 model, which has the highest context window of all the models by far (1M tokens)."

## Note

The article presents this as a comparative tool property, not as a measured task outcome.
```

### file: evidence-04.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-21-014
evidence_id: evidence-04
kind: method
citation: Render Blog, Aug. 12, 2025
source_url: https://render.com/blog/ai-coding-agents-benchmark
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
The benchmark used two test sets: a blank-repo Next.js app generation task and production tasks on a large Go API monorepo plus an Astro.js website.

## Source Excerpt

"To cover all use cases that an engineer would need in a production environment, I decided on two sets of tests:" followed by "A contrived control app, 100% “vibe coded” from a blank repo" and "A battery of tests against two huge code repos... The first is a large Go API monorepo, and the second is an Astro.js website."

## Note

The article describes these tests as covering both greenfield and existing-production-code use cases.
```

### file: evidence-05.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-21-014
evidence_id: evidence-05
kind: method
citation: Render Blog, Aug. 12, 2025
source_url: https://render.com/blog/ai-coding-agents-benchmark
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: bounded
contradiction_relevance: tension
---
The production-code comparison gave different tasks to different tools, and the author says scoring there was based primarily on overall impression rather than identical task execution.

## Source Excerpt

"There will, of course, be some variability in the tests because each tool was given a different task in each repo; however, I tried my best to give them an equal opportunity. The scoring on this section will be primarily based on my overall impression of the tool after working with it for some time."

## Note

This caveat limits strict like-for-like comparability across the production-task results.
```

### file: evidence-06.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-21-014
evidence_id: evidence-06
kind: result
citation: Render Blog, Aug. 12, 2025
source_url: https://render.com/blog/ai-coding-agents-benchmark
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
In the vibe-coding test, Cursor received 9/10 and required 3 follow-up error prompts.

## Source Excerpt

"Cursor (9/10)" and "Number of follow-up error prompts: 3."

## Note

This score applies to the article’s blank-repo Next.js app test.
```

### file: evidence-07.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-21-014
evidence_id: evidence-07
kind: result
citation: Render Blog, Aug. 12, 2025
source_url: https://render.com/blog/ai-coding-agents-benchmark
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
Cursor was the only tested agent that successfully created both a Docker Compose file with a database and a SQL migration script for the required table in the vibe-coding task.

## Source Excerpt

"It was the only agent to successfully create a Docker Compose file with a database, as well as a SQL migration script for the necessary table."

## Note

This statement is specific to the blank-repo app-generation benchmark.
```

### file: evidence-08.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-21-014
evidence_id: evidence-08
kind: result
citation: Render Blog, Aug. 12, 2025
source_url: https://render.com/blog/ai-coding-agents-benchmark
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
In the vibe-coding test, Claude Code received 7/10, required 4 follow-up error prompts, and the deployed functionality worked on the first try.

## Source Excerpt

"Claude Code (7/10)" and "Number of follow-up error prompts: 4." The source also states, "Once deployed, the functionality worked on the first try!"

## Note

The source also notes Claude struggled with some Next.js-specific issues before deployment.
```

### file: evidence-09.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-21-014
evidence_id: evidence-09
kind: result
citation: Render Blog, Aug. 12, 2025
source_url: https://render.com/blog/ai-coding-agents-benchmark
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
In the vibe-coding test, OpenAI Codex received 5/10, required 4 follow-up error prompts, and missed both Docker Compose and SQL migration scripts.

## Source Excerpt

"OpenAI Codex (5/10)" and "Number of follow-up error prompts: 4." The source adds, "It did however fail in some of the same places as Claude code: it missed the Docker Compose and SQL migration scripts."

## Note

The article also says Codex had trouble with some Next.js framework specifics.
```

### file: evidence-10.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-21-014
evidence_id: evidence-10
kind: result
citation: Render Blog, Aug. 12, 2025
source_url: https://render.com/blog/ai-coding-agents-benchmark
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
In the vibe-coding test, Gemini CLI received 3/10, required 7 follow-up error prompts, and did not display error or success messages in the app.

## Source Excerpt

"Gemini CLI (3/10)" and "Number of follow-up error prompts: 7." The source also states, "What is worse is that it did not display any error or success messages, so when troubleshooting the functionality, I had to rely on the server logs."

## Note

The source describes Gemini as needing guidance through multiple implementation and runtime issues in this test.
```

### file: evidence-11.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-21-014
evidence_id: evidence-11
kind: incident
citation: Render Blog, Aug. 12, 2025
source_url: https://render.com/blog/ai-coding-agents-benchmark
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: direct
contradiction_relevance: none
---
On the backend production task, Claude Code could not initially configure the go-quartz library and then switched to web search and GitHub documentation.

## Source Excerpt

"Claude came out of the gate strong with a good base implementation, but it had a hard time figuring out how to configure the go-quartz library. What was impressive was that once it realized that it couldn’t figure out the problem, it automatically switched to a web Search tool and reached out to the GitHub page to read the documentation."

## Note

This evidence concerns tool behavior during a specific Go backend task.
```

### file: evidence-12.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-21-014
evidence_id: evidence-12
kind: result
citation: Render Blog, Aug. 12, 2025
source_url: https://render.com/blog/ai-coding-agents-benchmark
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
On the backend production task, Cursor completed the refactor on the first try while matching existing dependency-injection and interface-returning patterns, but needed minor clarification about the Kubernetes namespace source.

## Source Excerpt

"The agent performed very well and completed the refactor on the first try with the proper context, keeping with our established patterns around Dependency Injection and factory functions, not returning structs, but interfaces..." The source also says, "I did have to follow up with a few minor clarifications, such as where it should get the Kubernetes namespace from."

## Note

The source presents this as performance on a specific refactor step in the Go repository.
```

### file: evidence-13.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-21-014
evidence_id: evidence-13
kind: result
citation: Render Blog, Aug. 12, 2025
source_url: https://render.com/blog/ai-coding-agents-benchmark
confidence: medium
mechanism_relevance: direct
contradiction_relevance: none
---
On the backend production task, Gemini CLI spent time loading broad codebase context and then completed the refactor on the first try with error handling.

## Source Excerpt

"it seems that Gemini was able to automatically load most, or at least all the relevant parts, of the codebase into the context window without my intervention. Because of this behavior, it took some time to read all the files and build context. But once it was satisfied, it pulled off the refactor on the first try, with proper error handling and everything."

## Note

The claimed broad context loading is based on the author's observation of tool behavior during use.
```

### file: evidence-14.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-21-014
evidence_id: evidence-14
kind: result
citation: Render Blog, Aug. 12, 2025
source_url: https://render.com/blog/ai-coding-agents-benchmark
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
On the backend production task, Codex’s connection-retry failsafe worked correctly on the first try, with one follow-up prompt used to align standards and formatting.

## Source Excerpt

"The model’s code accuracy was impressive... I only provided one follow-up prompt to refine the code to match our standards and formatting rules." The source then states, "Functionally, the code performed correctly on the first try."

## Note

This result refers to a NATS connection retry failsafe task in the same repository.
```

### file: evidence-15.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-21-014

The final benchmark table gives Cursor scores of Setup 9, Cost 5, Quality 9, Context 8, Integration 8, Speed 9, Specialized 8, with an average of 8.

In the table under "Final benchmarks for AI coding agents (2025)," the Cursor row lists: "9 5 9 8 8 9 8 8."

## Note

These values are presented as the article’s final summarized benchmark scores.
```

# Extractor Receipt

## Summary

This source contains firsthand comparative benchmark evidence about four AI coding agents, including setup details, task design, prompt counts, qualitative observations from specific coding tasks, and a final scored benchmark table.

## Evidence Files

### file: evidence-01.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-21-014
evidence_id: evidence-01
kind: method
citation: Render Blog, Aug. 12, 2025
source_url: https://render.com/blog/ai-coding-agents-benchmark
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
The comparison tested four tools: Claude Code, Cursor using Claude Sonnet 4, Gemini CLI, and OpenAI Codex.

## Source Excerpt

"As of mid-2025, there are more code agent AI tools than I can count; however, I selected the following four." The source then lists "Claude Code," "Cursor (using Claude Sonnet 4 for all tests)," "Gemini CLI," and "OpenAI Codex."

## Note

The source frames these four as a selected sample rather than an exhaustive market survey.
```

### file: evidence-02.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-21-014
evidence_id: evidence-02
kind: claim-support
citation: Render Blog, Aug. 12, 2025
source_url: https://render.com/blog/ai-coding-agents-benchmark
confidence: medium
mechanism_relevance: direct
contradiction_relevance: none
---
The source says Cursor uses a local-filesystem RAG-like system to gather codebase context, which could give it an advantage.

## Source Excerpt

"A point of interest: Cursor utilizes a RAG-like(Retrieval-Augmented Generation) system on the local filesystem to gather more context on the codebase, potentially giving it an advantage."

## Note

This is a source-stated product characteristic and possible advantage, not a separately verified measurement in the article.
```

### file: evidence-03.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-21-014
evidence_id: evidence-03

citation: Render Blog, Aug. 12, 2025
source_url: https://render.com/blog/ai-coding-agents-benchmark
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
The source states Gemini CLI used the Pro 2.5 model and had a 1M-token context window, the largest among the tested models.

## Source Excerpt

"Gemini CLI : Google’s offering in the code agent space offers the solid Pro 2.5 model, which has the highest context window of all the models by far (1M tokens)."

## Note

The article presents this as a comparative tool property, not as a measured task outcome.
```

### file: evidence-04.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-21-014
evidence_id: evidence-04
kind: method
citation: Render Blog, Aug. 12, 2025
source_url: https://render.com/blog/ai-coding-agents-benchmark
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
The benchmark used two test sets: a blank-repo Next.js app generation task and production tasks on a large Go API monorepo plus an Astro.js website.

## Source Excerpt

"To cover all use cases that an engineer would need in a production environment, I decided on two sets of tests:" followed by "A contrived control app, 100% “vibe coded” from a blank repo" and "A battery of tests against two huge code repos... The first is a large Go API monorepo, and the second is an Astro.js website."

## Note

The article describes these tests as covering both greenfield and existing-production-code use cases.
```

### file: evidence-05.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-21-014
evidence_id: evidence-05
kind: method
citation: Render Blog, Aug. 12, 2025
source_url: https://render.com/blog/ai-coding-agents-benchmark
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: bounded
contradiction_relevance: tension
---
The production-code comparison gave different tasks to different tools, and the author says scoring there was based primarily on overall impression rather than identical task execution.

## Source Excerpt

"There will, of course, be some variability in the tests because each tool was given a different task in each repo; however, I tried my best to give them an equal opportunity. The scoring on this section will be primarily based on my overall impression of the tool after working with it for some time."

## Note

This caveat limits strict like-for-like comparability across the production-task results.
```

---

## Source Excerpt

"Cursor (9/10)" and "Number of follow-up error prompts: 3."

## Note

This score applies to the article’s blank-repo Next.js app test.
```

### file: evidence-07.md
```markdown
---

---
Cursor was the only tested agent that successfully created both a Docker Compose file with a database and a SQL migration script for the required table in the vibe-coding task.

## Source Excerpt

"It was the only agent to successfully create a Docker Compose file with a database, as well as a SQL migration script for the necessary table."

## Note

This statement is specific to the blank-repo app-generation benchmark.
```

### file: evidence-08.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-21-014
evidence_id: evidence-08

---
In the vibe-coding test, Claude Code received 7/10, required 4 follow-up error prompts, and the deployed functionality worked on the first try.

## Source Excerpt

"Claude Code (7/10)" and "Number of follow-up error prompts: 4." The source also states, "Once deployed, the functionality worked on the first try!"

## Note

The source also notes Claude struggled with some Next.js-specific issues before deployment.
```

### file: evidence-09.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-21-014
evidence_id: evidence-09
kind: result
citation: Render Blog, Aug. 12, 2025
source_url: https://render.com/blog/ai-coding-agents-benchmark
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
In the vibe-coding test, OpenAI Codex received 5/10, required 4 follow-up error prompts, and missed both Docker Compose and SQL migration scripts.

## Source Excerpt

"OpenAI Codex (5/10)" and "Number of follow-up error prompts: 4." The source adds, "It did however fail in some of the same places as Claude code: it missed the Docker Compose and SQL migration scripts."

## Note

The article also says Codex had trouble with some Next.js framework specifics.
```

### file: evidence-10.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-21-014
evidence_id: evidence-10
kind: result

source_url: https://render.com/blog/ai-coding-agents-benchmark
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
In the vibe-coding test, Gemini CLI received 3/10, required 7 follow-up error prompts, and did not display error or success messages in the app.

## Source Excerpt

"Gemini CLI (3/10)" and "Number of follow-up error prompts: 7." The source also states, "What is worse is that it did not display any error or success messages, so when troubleshooting the functionality, I had to rely on the server logs."

## Note

The source describes Gemini as needing guidance through multiple implementation and runtime issues in this test.
```

### file: evidence-11.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-21-014
evidence_id: evidence-11
kind: incident
citation: Render Blog, Aug. 12, 2025
source_url: https://render.com/blog/ai-coding-agents-benchmark
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: direct
contradiction_relevance: none
---
On the backend production task, Claude Code could not initially configure the go-quartz library and then switched to web search and GitHub documentation.

## Source Excerpt

"Claude came out of the gate strong with a good base implementation, but it had a hard time figuring out how to configure the go-quartz library. What was impressive was that once it realized that it couldn’t figure out the problem, it automatically switched to a web Search tool and reached out to the GitHub page to read the documentation."

## Note

This evidence concerns tool behavior during a specific Go backend task.
```

### file: evidence-12.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-21-014
evidence_id: evidence-12
kind: result
citation: Render Blog, Aug. 12, 2025
source_url: https://render.com/blog/ai-coding-agents-benchmark
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
On the backend production task, Cursor completed the refactor on the first try while matching existing dependency-injection and interface-returning patterns, but needed minor clarification about the Kubernetes namespace source.

## Source Excerpt

"The agent performed very well and completed the refactor on the first try with the proper context, keeping with our established patterns around Dependency Injection and factory functions, not returning structs, but interfaces..." The source also says, "I did have to follow up with a few minor clarifications, such as where it should get the Kubernetes namespace from."

## Note

The source presents this as performance on a specific refactor step in the Go repository.
```

```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-21-014
evidence_id: evidence-13

---
On the backend production task, Gemini CLI spent time loading broad codebase context and then completed the refactor on the first try with error handling.

## Source Excerpt

"it seems that Gemini was able to automatically load most, or at least all the relevant parts, of the codebase into the context window without my intervention. Because of this behavior, it took some time to read all the files and build context. But once it was satisfied, it pulled off the refactor on the first try, with proper error handling and everything."

## Note

The claimed broad context loading is based on the author's observation of tool behavior during use.
```
